steps.title: Track the logic
steps.content: The text sets up a tempting question (how would a handwritten novel differ if typed?) and then blocks it: "each novel is a unique entity resulting from a specific set of circumstances." If each work is one-of-a-kind, there is no counterfactual version to compare it against.

steps.title: Predict the completion
steps.content: "Therefore" demands a conclusion that follows from uniqueness: you cannot meaningfully judge how the novel would have turned out by other means.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'authors... should instead work only on a computer': prescribes a behavior change the passage never argues for; overreach. ✗
  • Choice B — 'more success than those who work entirely on a computer': claims handwriting yields better novels, which the passage never supports; overreach. ✗
  • Choice C — 'less time to produce... than novels written on a computer': introduces a speed comparison absent from the passage; misread. ✗
  • Choice D — 'no way to reasonably evaluate how a work would be different': follows directly from each novel being a unique product of its circumstances. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
